headline: Apple's 50-year journey from garage to tech titan
summary: In early 1976 in California, Steve Wozniak had just completed the design of a computer circuit board he intended to share with fellow hobbyists at a prominent local club.  Apple's services business, which houses ​its App Store, Apple Music and its streaming service, has become a major ​driver of growth as its expanding base of devices brings in ​steady income from subscriptions and commissions on app sales.

content.title: Apple's 50-year journey from garage to tech titan
content.summary: In early 1976 in California, Steve Wozniak had just completed the design of a computer circuit board he intended to share with fellow hobbyists at a prominent local club.  Apple's services business, which houses ​its App Store, Apple Music and its streaming service, has become a major ​driver of growth as its expanding base of devices brings in ​steady income from subscriptions and commissions on app sales.

headline: Why Microsoft’s Growth Isn’t Saving Its Stock
summary: Microsoft (MSFT) is doing something unusual. It's beating earnings, growing revenue at 18% year-over-year, and yet its stock is down 36% from its peak. That doesn't happen to companies this size without a reason. The reason, in this case, is one number: capital expenditure (capex). Pre-AI era capex consistently ran at 25-27% of operating cash flow. The most recent full fiscal year saw that ratio climb to 47.4.

content.title: Why Microsoft’s Growth Isn’t Saving Its Stock
content.summary: Microsoft (MSFT) is doing something unusual. It's beating earnings, growing revenue at 18% year-over-year, and yet its stock is down 36% from its peak. That doesn't happen to companies this size without a reason. The reason, in this case, is one number: capital expenditure (capex). Pre-AI era capex consistently ran at 25-27% of operating cash flow. The most recent full fiscal year saw that ratio climb to 47.4.

headline: Big Tech: Time to buy?
summary: Rising costs versus strong demand. As the costs of AI grow, so does the debate over whether Big Tech stocks should have a place in your portfolio. On this episode of Trader Talk, Slatestone Wealth Partner and Chief Market Strategist, Kenny Polcari, Yahoo Finance Head of News, Myles Udland, Man Group Chief Market Strategist, Kristina Hooper, and Former Ellevest VP of People, Amanda Polcari, discuss what Kenny calls a huge opportunity.

headline: Amazon's cloud business in Bahrain damaged in Iran strike, FT reports
summary: Amazon's cloud computing operation in Bahrain was damaged after an Iranian ‌strike, the Financial Times reported on ‌Wednesday, citing a person familiar with the matter.  Bahrain's Interior Ministry ​said earlier in the day that civil defence teams were extinguishing a fire at a company facility following what authorities described as an ‌Iranian attack.  * The report of the strike came a day after Iran'sRevolutionary Guards threatened ​to target ​U.S. companies in theMiddle ​East, including Microsoft, ‌Google and Apple, inretaliation for attacks on Iran.

content.title: Amazon's cloud business in Bahrain damaged in Iran strike, FT reports

headline: Microsoft’s Thailand And Singapore AI Push Reshapes Long Term Investor Story
summary: Microsoft is committing $1b to cloud and AI infrastructure in Thailand as part of a new regional expansion push. The company is also planning a multi year $5.5b infrastructure investment in Singapore to grow data center and AI capacity. Both projects are tied to partnerships with local governments focused on AI skills training and workforce development. These moves aim to support large scale AI workloads while addressing data residency and digital sovereignty needs across Southeast...

headline: Analysis-SpaceX’s orbital data centers could face same hurdles as Microsoft’s abandoned undersea project
summary: SpaceX on Wednesday filed for an IPO that Elon Musk says will bankroll an effort to turn the rocket maker into an AI powerhouse, launching up to 1 million data‑center satellites into orbit to bypass power and water limits on Earth.  Microsoft had a similar ambition to escape land‑based computing constraints in 2015, when it lowered a shipping‑container‑sized data center onto the seabed off ‌Scotland, aiming to cut energy use through natural seawater cooling and tapping offshore wind and tidal power.  Microsoft’s "Project Natick," once touted as a potential breakthrough for the data‑center industry, successfully met all its technical targets but underwater data centers ‌were abandoned more than two years ago due to a lack of client demand and unviable economics, two sources with knowledge of the project told Reuters.

content.title: Analysis-SpaceX’s orbital data centers could face same hurdles as Microsoft’s abandoned undersea project
content.summary: SpaceX on Wednesday filed for an IPO that Elon Musk says will bankroll an effort to turn the rocket maker into an AI powerhouse, launching up to 1 million data‑center satellites into orbit to bypass power and water limits on Earth.  Microsoft had a similar ambition to escape land‑based computing constraints in 2015, when it lowered a shipping‑container‑sized data center onto the seabed off ‌Scotland, aiming to cut energy use through natural seawater cooling and tapping offshore wind and tidal power.  Microsoft’s "Project Natick," once touted as a potential breakthrough for the data‑center industry, successfully met all its technical targets but underwater data centers ‌were abandoned more than two years ago due to a lack of client demand and unviable economics, two sources with knowledge of the project told Reuters.

headline: Could Apple's next 50 years of success lie in AI, foldable phones?

Apple (AAPL) is celebrating its 50th anniversary on Wednesday, April 1. As tech consumers focus on the iPhone maker's latest wave of products, what could Apple's next 50 years look like?

Ramon Llamas, the IDC Research director for mobile devices and AR/VR, joins Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Josh Lipton on Asking for a Trend to discuss Apple's future in AI.
